candidates.title: README quickstart front-loading
candidates.source: The README quickstart currently starts at line ~197, below trust status, contributor onboarding, demo, provider preflight, provider capability, Why Atlas?, Broker-Neutral Model, System Status, What this is, What this is not, Current Development Status, Review and Feedback, and External Review sections. A new visitor must scroll through ~200 lines before seeing install/try instructions.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Quickstart appears immediately after the top status banner; a concise Try Atlas in 5 minutes subsection is present; the Review and Feedback link list is compressed to a curated set.
candidates.reason: Highest-impact onboarding improvement. Docs-only; no runtime changes.

candidates.title: Demo output expectations and discoverability
candidates.source: docs/demo-paper-workflow.md and docs/external-reviewer-walkthrough.md describe commands but do not show expected success output. The README notes No assets/atlas-demo.gif recording is checked in yet, which is honest but not constructive. Reviewers do not know what successful execution looks like.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Expected output snippets are added to demo docs and reviewer walkthrough; the README demo note is reframed as a constructive pointer to documented demos.
candidates.reason: Reduces reviewer uncertainty and support requests. Docs-only; no runtime changes.

candidates.title: Onboarding docs and checker stale version references
candidates.source: docs/development/onboarding.md contains a release_assurance.py example using v0.6.0 (line 99). scripts/check_onboarding_docs.py hardcodes public github release is 'v0.6.0' in REQUIRED_FACTS (line 145), which has been stale for multiple releases.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Onboarding doc release_assurance example references the current public release; checker REQUIRED_FACTS uses the current public release dynamically or is updated to match; tests pass.
candidates.reason: Prevents recurring contributor confusion from stale version references. No runtime changes.

candidates.title: Harden release-checklist automated version reference validation
candidates.source: docs/release-checklist.md contains version-specific examples (smoke tag scripts, version references) that drift after each release. A lightweight validation could catch stale examples earlier.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Checker validates that release-checklist examples reference the current public tag and source version without stale historical values.
candidates.reason: Valuable but broader than a minimal onboarding patch; defer to a dedicated docs-checker batch.

candidates.title: Demo GIF or screen recording asset
candidates.source: The README explicitly states No assets/atlas-demo.gif recording is checked in yet. A short ASCII or text-based demo recording could improve first impression.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: A safe, non-interactive demo recording is created and referenced from README.
candidates.reason: Media asset creation is out of patch scope and requires separate review for redaction/safety.

rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.title: Automatic skill activation
rejected.reason: Skill candidates remain manually reviewed and offline.

rejected.title: Automatic learning execution
rejected.reason: Learning suggestions remain offline and manually reviewed.

safety_boundaries: Live trading remains disabled by default.
Live submit remains disabled by default.
Provider execution remains disabled by default.
Broker execution remains disabled by default.
Risk gates remain deterministic and unchanged.
Human approval remains required for live order flow.
Credentials are not loaded unless explicitly configured.
No provider APIs, broker APIs, or real webhooks are called from CI or local checks.
constraints: Version is not bumped to 0.6.7 in this batch
No tag, GitHub release, or PyPI publish yet
release_criteria: dev_check, ci_check, and release_check --quick pass locally
CI on main is green after the prep commit is pushed
Version is bumped to 0.6.7 in pyproject.toml and src/atlas_agent/__init__.py
scripts/check_v067_release_prep.py and matching tests are created
Owner approval is obtained for tag cutover
